visuals.title: The Critical Section Problem
visuals.description: Animated diagram covering race conditions, CS structure (Entry/CS/Exit/Remainder), the three requirements (Mutual Exclusion, Progress, Bounded Waiting), Peterson's Solution, and hardware solutions (Test-and-Set, CAS)

diagrams.title: Race Condition Example
diagrams.description: counter = 5, P1: counter++, P2: counter--. Expected result is 5, but actual could be 4, 5, or 6 due to interleaved execution (lost update problem).

diagrams.title: Critical Section Structure
diagrams.description: Entry Section -> Critical Section -> Exit Section -> Remainder Section (loop)

diagrams.title: Three Requirements for a Valid Solution
diagrams.description: Mutual Exclusion, Progress, and Bounded Waiting

requirements.name: Mutual Exclusion
requirements.rule: Only one process in CS at a time

requirements.name: Progress
requirements.rule: No indefinite postponement; only non-remainder processes participate in decision

requirements.name: Bounded Waiting
requirements.rule: A bound exists on entries by others after a process requests CS

diagrams.title: Peterson's Solution (2 processes)
diagrams.description: Software-based mutual exclusion for two processes using flag[] and turn

data.invariant: At most one process in CS
data.entry: flag[i]=true, turn=j, wait while flag[j] && turn==j

solutions.name: Test-and-Set
solutions.mechanism: Atomically read old value, set to true
